skill.name: github-pr-creation
skill.description: Guides GitHub pull request creation by checking branch context, reviewing commits, running tests, drafting Conventional Commits titles, and suggesting labels.
skill.summary: Prepare GitHub pull requests with commit analysis, test checks, and structured PR text.
skill.author: Bae-ChangHyun

security_audit.summary: The external command findings are mostly true positives for intended git, GitHub CLI, and test execution workflows. The weak cryptography findings are false positives caused by ordinary words such as description. The skill also uses prompt-control language in its description, so publication should wait for safer wording and command safeguards.

high_findings.title: Prompt-Control Language in Skill Metadata
high_findings.description: The skill description says the skill must be used and that it overrides default PR creation behavior. This attempts to control assistant behavior from untrusted skill content, although it does not request security bypass.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains direct prompt-control terms, including MUST and OVERRIDES. Confidence is high because the wording is explicit, but it is scoped to PR workflow behavior.

medium_findings.title: Side-Effecting GitHub and Git Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run git and GitHub CLI commands, including creating a pull request. This is legitimate for the skill purpose but can change remote project state if used without careful confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and include gh pr create. The skill includes approval rules, so the risk is operational rather than clearly malicious.

medium_findings.title: Repository-Controlled Test Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill tells agents to run make test, npm test, or pytest. These commands may execute code from the current repository, which is risky when the repository is untrusted.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The test commands are shown directly and commonly run project-defined code. The risk depends on repository trust and execution environment.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography alerts do not correspond to cryptographic code. The cited lines contain metadata and PR title guidance, not DES, MD5, SHA1, or similar algorithm use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines were reviewed and contain no cryptographic operation. The alerts appear to match ordinary words such as description.

low_findings.title: Shell Command Substitution Requires Careful Quoting
low_findings.description: The PR creation template uses shell command substitution with a heredoc to build the body. This preserves formatting but remains shell-sensitive if adapted with unsafe dynamic content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command substitution is visible and legitimate for formatting. Risk is lower because the heredoc delimiter is quoted and the body is a template placeholder.

dangerous_patterns.title: External CLI Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill repeatedly instructs use of git, gh, make, npm, and pytest commands. These can inspect local state, run project code, or modify GitHub state.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly documented and relevant to the skill. They are not hidden, but they require user confirmation and trusted repositories.

dangerous_patterns.title: Prompt Override Wording
dangerous_patterns.description: The metadata uses mandatory override language that can compete with platform or user instructions. Marketplace publication should prefer neutral activation language.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact terms appear in the skill description. The concern is semantic prompt control, not executable code.

content.user_title: Create GitHub Pull Requests With Review Context
content.value_statement: Preparing a pull request can miss branch checks, test evidence, and clear review notes. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through a structured PR workflow.
actual_capabilities: Checks the current branch, base branch, commit list, file changes, and repository status.
Prompts for target branch confirmation before creating a pull request.
Suggests PR titles that follow Conventional Commits style.
Drafts a PR body with summary, changes, test plan, and related work.
Suggests labels based on commit type and available repository labels.
Requires tests and user approval before running the PR creation command.
limitations: Requires GitHub CLI to be installed and authenticated.
Assumes common branch flows such as feature to develop or develop to main.
Cannot guarantee project tests are safe in untrusted repositories.
Does not merge, review, or update existing pull requests.

use_cases.title: Prepare Feature PRs
use_cases.description: Convert feature branch commits into a clear PR title, summary, change list, and test plan.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Reviews
use_cases.description: Use one repeatable checklist before opening pull requests across a team repository.

use_cases.title: Create Release PRs
use_cases.description: Draft release pull requests from develop to main with branch confirmation and concise release notes.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic PR
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a standard pull request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a GitHub pull request for my current branch. Confirm the target branch before creating it.

prompt_templates.title: Draft Without Publishing
prompt_templates.scenario: Review the proposed PR content before any remote action.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my branch and draft the PR title, body, labels, and test plan. Do not create the PR yet.

prompt_templates.title: Use a Specific Base Branch
prompt_templates.scenario: A team uses a non-default branch flow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a PR from my current branch into develop. Include commit analysis, changed files, and test results.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Release PR
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced release workflow with stronger review context.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a release PR from develop to main after checking commits, task references, labels, and passing tests.

output_examples.input: Create a PR for this feature branch.
output_examples.output: The assistant confirms the base branch, summarizes commits, reports test status, drafts a Conventional Commits title, and shows the PR body for approval.

output_examples.input: Draft the PR but do not open it.
output_examples.output: The assistant provides a proposed title, summary, change list, test plan, related references, and label suggestions without changing GitHub state.

output_examples.input: Open a release PR from develop to main.
output_examples.output: The assistant verifies the branch direction, reviews release commits, prepares release-focused notes, and asks for confirmation before using GitHub CLI.

best_practices: Use it only in repositories where local test commands are trusted.
Confirm the base branch and PR content before running GitHub CLI commands.
Review generated labels and related task references before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not run it on untrusted repositories without isolating test execution.
Do not skip branch confirmation when creating release or hotfix PRs.
Do not use it to bypass team review templates or required checks.

faq.question: Does this skill create the pull request automatically?
faq.answer: It can create a pull request with GitHub CLI, but it instructs the assistant to show content and ask for approval first.

faq.question: What tools does it require?
faq.answer: It requires git and GitHub CLI. Project tests may require tools such as make, npm, or pytest.

faq.question: Can it choose the correct base branch?
faq.answer: It suggests common branch flows and asks the user to confirm the target branch.

faq.question: Does it support Conventional Commits?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses commit type and scope information to suggest a Conventional Commits PR title.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted repositories?
faq.answer: Use caution. Test commands may execute repository code, so untrusted repositories should be isolated.

faq.question: Can it merge or review pull requests?
faq.answer: No. This skill focuses on creating new pull requests, not merging or reviewing existing ones.
